name: Flatten PTX Hot-Band Compute Helpers To Reduce Register Pressure

summary.name: Flatten PTX Hot-Band Compute Helpers To Reduce Register Pressure
summary.hypothesis: The round-5 control-path tweak effectively held runtime flat while leaving the same occupancy-limited signature in place: the hot PTX kernel still reports only 16.49% active warps and the same register-limited CTA ceiling. That makes register pressure the most coherent next family. The historically rehydrated helper-flattening branch already showed a 24.696832 ms run, and the current profile still supports its thesis that the PTX helper structure is keeping too much live state around the active 128x128 hot-band path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and weak latency hiding caused by helper-induced live ranges in the PTX hot-band compute path.
summary.risk: Medium. Helper flattening is still bounded to the active PTX branch, but it can devolve into a no-op if ptxas keeps the same live ranges and scheduling.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.177664 ms accepted base
ranking_notes: The latest flat result specifically weakens copy-order retunes and strengthens the case for attacking the unchanged register ceiling directly.
This family is already the highest-ranked rehydrated representative in the live frontier after the still-open PTX control family.
It is ranked first because it is the most defensible next move that still stays on the active PTX branch.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if register count and active-warps remain pinned after one bounded helper-flattening pass, or if runtime still behaves like noise around the current flat plateau.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
